tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    quantify() Function

    quantify.Rd

    Unified wrapper function for evaluating fault trees. Evaluates whether the top event occurs (binary evaluation) or computes top event failure probability (probabilistic evaluation) based on the prob parameter.

    quantify(f, newdata, prob = FALSE)

    Arguments

    f

    (Required) Function from formulate(), with one argument per basic event. Used to identify event names and to evaluate the fault tree.

    newdata

    (Required) Data for evaluation. When prob = FALSE, this represents binary scenarios (0/1 or TRUE/FALSE) for each basic event. When prob = TRUE, this represents failure probabilities (0 to 1) for each basic event. Can be a tibble/data frame with one column per basic event, a single vector/list, or (for prob = TRUE) a matrix. See details below.

    prob

    (Optional) Logical. If FALSE (default), performs binary evaluation using quantify_binary(). If TRUE, computes failure probability using quantify_prob().

    Value

    A vector of outcomes. The type depends on prob:

    • If prob = FALSE: Returns a logical vector (TRUE = system failure, FALSE = no failure). If newdata is a data frame with multiple rows, returns a vector with one outcome per row. If newdata is a single scenario, returns a single logical value.

    • If prob = TRUE: Returns a numeric vector of top event failure probabilities. If newdata is a single scenario, returns a single numeric value. If newdata has multiple rows, returns a numeric vector with one probability per scenario.

    Both modes return vectors in predict()-style format.

    Details

    This function provides a unified interface for both binary and probabilistic fault tree evaluation:

    • Binary evaluation (prob = FALSE): Uses quantify_binary() to evaluate whether the top event occurs given binary event states. Each event is 0 (did not occur) or 1 (occurred). No probabilities are used.

    • Probabilistic evaluation (prob = TRUE): Uses quantify_prob() to compute the top event failure probability from basic event failure probabilities. Assumes independent basic events and uses the complete truth table for exact computation (O(2^n) complexity).

    When prob = FALSE, newdata can be:

    • A data frame with one column per basic event (names from formalArgs(f))

    • A single vector/list (unnamed = positional, or named)

    • Values are logical (TRUE/FALSE) or numeric (0/1)

    When prob = TRUE, newdata can be:

    • A single vector or list (one scenario) with probabilities in [0, 1]

    • A data frame or matrix with one row per scenario and columns for each basic event (names must match formalArgs(f))

    • Unnamed vectors are interpreted in formalArgs(f) order

    See also

    formulate for creating the function, calculate for the full truth table, quantify_binary for binary evaluation, quantify_prob for probabilistic evaluation

    Examples

    library(tidyverse)
library(tidyfault)
data("fakenodes")
data("fakeedges")

f <- curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
  equate() %>%
  formulate()

# Binary evaluation (default): single scenario
f %>% quantify(c(T, T, T, F))
#> [1] TRUE
f %>% quantify(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE))
#> [1] FALSE

# Binary evaluation: multiple scenarios
scenarios_tbl <- tibble(
  A = c(1L, 0L, 1L),
  B = c(0L, 1L, 1L),
  C = c(1L, 0L, 0L),
  D = c(0L, 1L, 1L)
)
f %>% quantify(scenarios_tbl)
#> [1] FALSE FALSE  TRUE

# Probabilistic evaluation: single scenario
f %>% quantify(c(0.1, 0.2, 0.05, 0.15), prob = TRUE)
#> [1] 0.01285

# Probabilistic evaluation: multiple scenarios
probs_df <- as.data.frame(replicate(4, runif(100), simplify = FALSE))
names(probs_df) <- formalArgs(f)
f %>% quantify(probs_df, prob = TRUE)
#>   [1] 0.309464679 0.598150861 0.461376509 0.066705990 0.236247800 0.002390729
#>   [7] 0.829554435 0.094956967 0.389435890 0.224270576 0.646778459 0.099067529
#>  [13] 0.913010945 0.021696354 0.152824101 0.578668702 0.734242450 0.046353020
#>  [19] 0.472915660 0.573248099 0.359835069 0.230429870 0.264034509 0.653155655
#>  [25] 0.154530180 0.026803841 0.043557939 0.385782036 0.091716632 0.032981226
#>  [31] 0.033761079 0.559419136 0.206684790 0.723611617 0.148762629 0.860696485
#>  [37] 0.156296678 0.370774455 0.462822303 0.073178731 0.762502383 0.506803981
#>  [43] 0.055263843 0.110450013 0.068032185 0.643049355 0.482361435 0.180682680
#>  [49] 0.204185206 0.927287294 0.084361917 0.662351605 0.278966930 0.040357748
#>  [55] 0.438501863 0.377007103 0.035592988 0.392616148 0.250347149 0.313615097
#>  [61] 0.608389356 0.504608985 0.463267860 0.013728016 0.272791792 0.213211383
#>  [67] 0.868355438 0.407669996 0.589712016 0.495955616 0.440147685 0.129845958
#>  [73] 0.480150241 0.323482098 0.459635185 0.103129148 0.091807266 0.789335095
#>  [79] 0.193544069 0.002776052 0.568641640 0.152536300 0.080615960 0.610902890
#>  [85] 0.160639082 0.742495579 0.328081342 0.271691206 0.090089890 0.378799957
#>  [91] 0.130114130 0.627155107 0.663129410 0.151456526 0.086191280 0.833854018
#>  [97] 0.385753454 0.295293912 0.765847166 0.049679954
